name: Buckle Arrestor Spacing
expected_response_detailed: Offshore pipeline: buckle arrestor spacing based on propagation pressure. Propagation pressure 2,500 psi for 24-inch X65. Arrestor required within 1,000 feet of potential buckle sources (fishing gear, anchors). Design: concrete or steel sleeves. Spacing formula: P_prop/γ_water - external pressure margin. Limit state design: 1.1 safety factor on propagation. Installation: over pipe without girth weld interference, cathodic protection continuity or isolation depending on design.

name: Slug Catcher Sizing
expected_response_detailed: Slug catcher vessel: inlet separator for two-phase flow. Volume based on maximum expected slug from pigging operation. 1,000 barrels liquid holdup for offshore, 2-4 hours residence time onshore. Diameter selected for vapor velocity (0.5-1.5 ft/sec). Liquid level control: 30-60% operating range. Instrumentation: level transmitter, pressure safety relief, temperature, densitometer for liquid composition. Per API 14E.

name: Pipe Support Spacing Calculation
expected_response_detailed: API 570 spacing: span = √(0.33 × E × I / w) where E modulus, I moment of inertia, w distributed load. 24-inch schedule 40: span approximately 25 feet for liquid service, 35 feet gas. Account for concentrated loads (valves). Allowable deflection 0.5 inch. Thermal expansion guide spacing: full guide every second support. Shoe height for insulation maintenance.

name: Strain Based Design Limitations
expected_response_detailed: SBD for extreme terrain: tensile strain limit 0.5% typical, 2.5% with deformation control. Compressive strain: 0.2% wrinkle initiation, 0.5-1% acceptance with wrinkles monitored. Notched bar compression test for girth weld. Bending strain monitoring during installation. Ovality limit 3% per API 5L. Crack arrest required if defect tolerance lower than flaw size.

name: Gas Dehydration Unit Design
expected_response_detailed: Glycol dehydration: TEG circulation rate 2-5 gallons per lb water removed. Reboiler 400°F max (TEG degradation). Stripping gas if <7 lb/MMscf specification. Regenerator tray efficiency. Flash tank 75 psig minimum, 15-minute retention. Filter for hydrocarbon removal. pH adjustment for corrosion. Glycol losses 0.05-0.1 gallon/MMscf.

name: Pipeline Surge Analysis
expected_response_detailed: Water hammer for liquid pipelines: Joukowsky equation ΔP = ρ × a × ΔV. Wave speed a = √(1/ρ × (1/K + D/(t×E))). Surge pressure can exceed 2× operating. Mitigation: surge tanks, controlled valve closure (10+ seconds), check valves with dashpots, pressure relief. Transient software (OLGA, PipelineStudio) for modeling. Worst case: ESD instantaneous closure.

name: Cathodic Protection Rectifier Sizing
expected_response_detailed: Rectifier current output: based on coating quality and surface area. New FBE coating: 0.1-0.5 mA/sq ft. Poor coating: 2-10 mA/sq ft. Voltage output: overcome soil resistivity and anode bed resistance. Typical pipe ground bed: 50-amp output, 24 volts. Deep anode beds for congested areas. Remote monitoring via SCADA. Transformer-rectifier efficiency 70%. Reference electrode calibration annually per NACE SP0169.

name: Block Valve Spacing per Class
expected_response_detailed: 192.745 spacing: Class 1 (rural) 10 miles, Class 2 (suburban) 7.5 miles, Class 3 (towns) 4 miles, Class 4 (urban) 2.5 miles. Exceptions: navigable waterways every crossing, HCA within limits, offshore platforms. Automatic or remote-controlled for Class 3/4. Manual acceptable Class 1/2 if accessible within 1 hour. Test frequency: partial stroke monthly, full stroke annually. Location markers required.

name: Gas Quality Specification Limits
expected_response_detailed: Pipeline tariff specifications: H2S <0.25-1 grain/100 scf (4-16 ppm), CO2 <2-3%, moisture dew point 20°F below coldest operating, heating value 950-1,150 Btu/scf, hydrocarbon dew point line conditions (no liquids), O2 <10 ppm, total sulfur <5-20 grains. Wobbe index for interchangeability. Delivery specifications in interconnect agreements per GPA 2261.

name: Pipeline Re-Rating Procedure
expected_response_detailed: Re-rating per 192.619: if test data available new MAOP = test pressure / 1.25. If older than 5 years: 85% of test or 80% of MAOP if never pressure tested. Engineering analysis for wall thickness, material properties, elevation profile, class location changes. Pressure reduction notification to PHMSA within 5 days. Customer notification if contractual MAOP affected. Records updated to reflect new MAOP.
